title: Phanera franckii
intro: Phanera franckii, also known as Franck's tree or Franck's phanera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Phanera franckii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phanera franckii is often used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, extracts, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Phanera franckii is classified within the genus Phanera, which is a part of the family Fabaceae. This classification places Phanera franckii within the legume family. The genus Phanera is further grouped into the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. The Caesalpinioideae subfamily is one of the six subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. The exact classification can be summarized as Phanera franckii, subfamily Caesalpinioideae, family Fabaceae.
